schema_note: 6 hook types × 4 visual variants. Each variant is a complete image direction brief. performance_data is populated by creative-performance-updater.ts after paid results flow in.

pain_point.label: Pain Point
pain_point.visual_strategy: Show the emotional texture of the pain — not the gym, not the solution. The viewer should feel recognised, not sold to.

A.scene: Person alone on a back porch or park bench, late afternoon. Not looking at camera — looking slightly off-frame. Coffee cup that has gone cold. Comfortable clothes. No gym imagery anywhere.
A.subject: Woman or man, 40-48, real proportions, dressed in everyday clothes — not activewear
A.emotion: quiet exhaustion and wistfulness — the feeling of having been meaning to do something for a long time
A.lighting: golden hour or soft overcast — warm but not bright
A.composition: Two-thirds portrait. Subject fills left two-thirds. Right third is soft background — blurred greenery or suburban neighbourhood.
A.do_not_show: gym equipment, workout clothes, anyone in shape, before/after visual cues, staged poses, camera-facing smiles

B.scene: Close shot of hands — wrapped around a coffee mug or resting on a kitchen table. No face visible. A phone or planner visible in soft background, slightly out of focus.
B.subject: Hands only — no face. Adult hands, aged 35-55, not manicured, real daily-life wear
B.emotion: still, reflective — the quiet moment before the day starts when the feeling lands
B.lighting: soft indoor morning light — window light, warm kitchen tones
B.composition: Tight crop. Hands centred. Background depth-of-field blurred. No text in frame.
B.do_not_show: gym gear, fitness imagery, rings or jewellery as focal point, screens with content visible

C.scene: Parent watching a child play in the background — playground or backyard. Parent is seated or leaning against something. Not participating, watching. Distance between parent and child matters — it conveys the gap.
C.subject: Parent, 38-50, in casual clothes, real body type — not a fitness-level physique
C.emotion: love mixed with quiet guilt — the feeling of not being able to keep up
C.lighting: natural outdoor light, afternoon — warm and real
C.composition: Parent in foreground slightly soft, child in background slightly soft. Both in frame but separated.
C.do_not_show: gym equipment, athletic wear, parent and child playing together actively, anyone posed

D.scene: Person looking at themselves in a bathroom mirror — not dramatically, just a moment of noticing. Not posed. The reflection shows someone pausing, not posing.
D.subject: Woman or man, 40-50, in casual morning clothes — t-shirt or robe, real proportions
D.emotion: recognition without drama — the quiet moment of noticing, not despair
D.lighting: bathroom morning light — slightly warm, soft overhead or window light
D.composition: We see them and their reflection. Neither fills the frame entirely. Intimate and private.
D.do_not_show: dramatic before/after posing, body isolation shots (stomach/arms/thighs), exaggerated expressions, gym branding in mirror

curiosity_gap.label: Curiosity Gap
curiosity_gap.visual_strategy: Create visual mystery — something is happening just out of frame, or a contrast is implied without being resolved. The viewer wants to know what comes next.

A.scene: Person stopped mid-stride on a street or parking lot, looking at something we cannot see. Their expression shows recognition or realisation — not fear, not surprise, just the look of someone who just understood something.
A.subject: Adult, 35-50, casual clothes, paused in motion
A.emotion: moment of realisation — a thought landing
A.lighting: natural outdoor daylight — overcast or late afternoon
A.composition: Wide enough to show context but close enough to read expression. Subject slightly off-centre.
A.do_not_show: gym, fitness context, anything being advertised, phone in hand

B.scene: A door partially open — any interior threshold, setting intentionally ambiguous. Warm light coming through the gap. Person's silhouette visible at the threshold but not fully inside. No gym signage or equipment visible.
B.subject: Silhouette only — gender and age ambiguous
B.emotion: threshold moment — between before and after, not yet decided
B.lighting: backlit from the warm interior, cooler tone outside
B.composition: Door takes most of the frame. Silhouette is the only human element. Dramatic but not overdone.
B.do_not_show: gym equipment visible through door, gym signage, anyone athletic, any text in scene

C.scene: Phone screen visible in someone's hand — not showing the content clearly, just that they have paused on something. Their face is not shown but their body language suggests they are considering something seriously.
C.subject: Hands holding phone, partial wrist and arm visible, casual sleeve
C.emotion: consideration — the moment before a decision
C.lighting: soft indoor light, phone glow mixing with ambient warm light
C.composition: Phone centred, screen content blurred or unreadable, surrounding context soft
C.do_not_show: readable screen content, gym app, anything that reveals the ad context, gym branding

D.scene: Two people in conversation inside a gym — gym is background context only. One person gesturing, the other listening and leaning in. Warm/retargeting audiences only (awareness level 4+) — do not use for cold campaigns.
D.subject: Coach or staff member and a member in mid-conversation — neither is athletic-looking
D.emotion: engaged, attentive — something important is being said
D.lighting: natural gym light from windows, warm and grounded
D.composition: Medium shot. Both in frame. Faces partially visible but not the focal point — the body language is the story.
D.do_not_show: gym equipment as focal point, anyone working out, fitness bodies, staged coaching poses

bold_claim.label: Bold Claim
bold_claim.visual_strategy: The image supports the specificity of the claim — it looks real, operational, trustworthy. The creative direction focuses on evidence of systems and care, not excitement.

A.scene: Coach writing on a whiteboard or reviewing a printed plan with a member — the plan is visible but blurred. The act of planning is the content, not what is written. Alternative grounded setting: person standing in their kitchen in the morning, still in pajamas, looking at their phone with one eye barely open. Kitchen counter with dishes from last night still visible — real morning, not curated.
A.subject: Coach, 35-50, clearly competent but not intimidating. Member looking at the plan with interest.
A.emotion: collaborative, purposeful — this is what having a real system looks like
A.lighting: clean gym lighting — functional and honest, not dramatic
A.composition: Medium shot. Coach and plan both in frame. Member partially in frame — showing this is for them.
A.do_not_show: workout in progress, athletic bodies, before/after framing, anyone posed for the camera

B.scene: Close-up of a text message thread on a phone — the thread shows regular two-way messages, clearly ongoing check-ins. The content of messages is not readable but the rhythm of back-and-forth is visible.
B.subject: Phone in hand, no face shown
B.lighting: soft indoor light mixed with phone screen glow
B.composition: Phone fills most of frame. Thread fills screen. Clean, simple, honest.
B.do_not_show: readable message content that makes specific claims, gym branding, fitness imagery

C.scene: Split scene or sequential diptych — Day 1 (person walking in, uncertain) and Day 30 (same person, easier body language, familiar with the space). No visible body change — the change is in ease and belonging.
C.subject: Same person, real body type — the change is posture and ease, not physique
C.emotion: quiet transformation — not dramatic, just someone who now belongs here
C.lighting: consistent between panels — same gym, different energy
C.composition: Two equal panels side by side. Clean centre divide. No before/after labels.
C.do_not_show: body composition change, weight, anything that suggests physique transformation, dramatic expressions

D.scene: Calendar or planner with consistent check-marks across 30 days — real paper planner, not digital, showing a streak of attendance. A coffee cup nearby. Normal desk environment.
D.subject: No person visible — just the evidence of showing up
D.emotion: satisfaction of consistency — the habit has formed
D.lighting: natural desk light — window light, morning feel
D.composition: Calendar centred. Coffee cup as supporting element. Clean, organised, personal.
D.do_not_show: gym branding on calendar, any specific dates or names visible, digital app screenshot

relatability.label: Relatability
relatability.visual_strategy: The image should feel like something the viewer would share with a friend. It captures a universal experience without naming it as a gym ad. High share potential.

A.scene: Gym bag sitting by a front door, untouched — still packed from a week ago. A sticky note on top. The rest of the scene is the normal chaos of daily life. Alternative: person sitting in their car after work, engine off, not ready to go inside yet — workplace parking lot visible through windshield, fluorescent building light coming through the glass.
A.subject: No person — just the still life of good intentions
A.emotion: wry recognition — everyone has done this
A.lighting: everyday home light — hallway or entrance area
A.composition: Bag centred in foreground. Home context visible in background, slightly out of focus.
A.do_not_show: brand logos on bag, gym equipment, anything aspirational — this is the relatable fail, not the solution

B.scene: Alarm going off on a phone — 5:30am — and a hand reaching to silence it. The person is clearly not getting up for the gym. Bedside context is visible. Real and honest.
B.subject: Hand reaching to phone, bedside visible — unmistakably a snooze moment
B.emotion: universal failure — nobody gets up at 5:30 without accountability
B.lighting: dark bedroom with phone screen as primary light source
B.composition: Phone centred, alarm time clearly visible but not branded, bedside soft focus
B.do_not_show: gym content on phone, athletic imagery, aspirational morning context

C.scene: Person scrolling through their phone on the couch, TV on in background, evening light. Not sedentary in a judgmental way — just a normal evening that replaced what was supposed to be a gym visit.
C.subject: Adult, 38-50, casual home clothes, relaxed but slightly distracted
C.emotion: comfortable inertia — the pull of not going that everyone understands
C.lighting: warm TV glow and lamp light — cosy but a bit numbing
C.composition: Medium shot. Couch context clear. TV visible in background, out of focus. Person is real, not staged.
C.do_not_show: phone screen content visible, gym on TV, anyone athletic, judgmental framing

D.scene: Person checking themselves in a dressing room or shop mirror while trying on clothes — the expression is not horror, just a familiar recognition. Normal clothes, not gym wear.
D.subject: Adult, 38-52, in regular clothing being tried on, real body proportions
D.emotion: quiet recognition — the kind that happens when fitting rooms are honest
D.lighting: dressing room lighting — slightly harsh fluorescent mixed with warm ambient
D.composition: Person and reflection both in frame. Intimate. Private moment made relatable.
D.do_not_show: dramatic expressions, scale, measuring tape, anything clinical or before/after adjacent

pattern_interrupt.label: Pattern Interrupt
pattern_interrupt.visual_strategy: Subvert the viewer's expectation of what a gym ad looks like. Use the opposite of every visual cue they expect. The interruption is the point.

A.scene: Person sitting at their kitchen table in the early morning, fully dressed and ready to leave, but just staring out the window at nothing. Coffee cup going cold beside them. Not sad, not energised — just in the in-between moment of stillness before the day has any demands. No gym context anywhere in the scene.
A.subject: Adult, 38-52, dressed for the day in work clothes or smart casual — sitting quietly. Not in athletic wear.
A.emotion: pre-decision stillness — the moment before the narrative of the day begins
A.lighting: soft early morning window light, warm but quiet, kitchen ambient
A.composition: Medium shot. Person at left third of frame. Window and outside visible on right, soft focus. Coffee cup in foreground slightly out of focus.
A.do_not_show: gym equipment, athletic gear, fitness context, motivational imagery, anything that reads as a gym ad

B.scene: Person mid-sentence talking to someone off camera, caught in the middle of saying something true about themselves. They are not performing — this is a candid moment of honest conversation caught on camera. Kitchen or living room setting, daytime.
B.subject: Adult, 38-52, casual everyday clothes, mid-conversation — face partially turned, animated expression, completely unaware of the camera
B.emotion: candid honesty — the look of someone saying something they actually mean, not something they rehearsed
B.lighting: kitchen or living room, daytime, natural window light — no overhead fluorescent, no ring light, no studio setup
B.composition: Three-quarter portrait. Person slightly off-centre. Off-camera conversational partner implied by their gaze and body language.
B.do_not_show: gym exterior, gym parking lot, anxious body-related expressions, anyone in workout gear, any fitness context, staged conversation, direct camera eye contact

C.scene: Two people at a coffee shop or kitchen table — not a gym — but one is clearly the coach and the other the member, mid-conversation. The conversation is happening in ordinary life, not in a gym context.
C.subject: Coach-like figure and a member-like figure in everyday clothes, in a regular setting
C.emotion: relationship and trust — this is what a real coaching relationship looks like, not a training session
C.lighting: coffee shop or kitchen — warm, natural, lived-in
C.composition: Equal frame for both people. The connection between them is the visual subject.
C.do_not_show: gym equipment, workout clothes, anything that signals a gym ad at first glance

D.scene: Empty gym at 6am — all equipment present but nobody working out. Just the space itself, quiet and waiting. Clean but lived-in. Not a studio shoot. Warm/retargeting audiences only (awareness level 4+) — do not use for cold campaigns.
D.subject: No people — just the space
D.emotion: anticipation without pressure — this place exists for you when you are ready
D.lighting: early morning gym light — a few lights on, some natural light beginning, atmospheric
D.composition: Wide shot of gym floor. Depth and space are the subject. Equipment is context, not featured.
D.do_not_show: people working out, motivational posters visible, aggressive lighting, any stock-photo gym feel

identity_shift.label: Identity Shift
identity_shift.visual_strategy: Show the identity the prospect wants — not as a distant aspiration but as something already beginning. This is the resolution creative. Used after pain_point has landed.

A.scene: Person walking into a gym alone, confident — not posing, just walking in with the ease of someone who belongs there now. Gym entrance, morning or late afternoon. Real person, not athletic.
A.subject: Adult, 40-50, casual gym-appropriate clothes but not athletic gear — just someone who goes now
A.emotion: quiet ownership — this is just what they do now
A.lighting: warm gym entrance light — welcoming, not dramatic
A.composition: Shot from behind or three-quarter view — we see them entering, not posing for us.
A.do_not_show: dramatic transformation, athletic body, anyone posed at camera, motivational pose

B.scene: Person keeping up with their kids — outdoor activity, park, hiking trail. The adult is clearly able to participate now. Not a fitness ad — a life moment. The child and parent are in it together.
B.subject: Parent, 38-52, with child aged 6-14, both in motion together
B.emotion: the resolution the avatar actually wants — not a six-pack, being able to keep up
B.composition: Both parent and child in frame, parent is clearly participating not watching, joyful and real
B.do_not_show: gym imagery, workout context, athletic bodies, any staged family stock photo feel

C.scene: Person in a locker room, finished with a workout — not pumped and sweaty in a dramatic way, just done. Sitting, normal clothes back on, a small expression of quiet satisfaction. Everyday, not theatrical.
C.subject: Adult, 38-52, getting ready to leave after a session — the small satisfaction of having gone
C.emotion: low-key accomplishment — this is what it feels like to be someone who goes
C.lighting: locker room light — functional, honest, not flattering but real
C.composition: Medium shot. Context of locker room clear. Expression is subtle — not a victory pose.
C.do_not_show: dramatic sweat, athletic body showcase, mirror selfie pose, before/after framing

D.scene: Morning routine — person dressed and ready to leave, gym bag by the door, coffee in hand. The bag is packed and going with them this time. Normal home context. Alternative: person looking in the bathroom mirror while brushing teeth — not dramatically, just the ordinary morning mirror moment. Bathroom with real clutter visible — toothpaste on counter, everyday products, real bathroom not staged.
D.subject: Adult, 38-52, in everyday clothes, ready to head out — the bag is going with them
D.emotion: this is just what mornings look like now — habit without drama
D.lighting: morning home light — warm kitchen or hallway
D.composition: Person and bag both in frame. Coffee as a normalising element. Clean and real.
D.do_not_show: dramatic transformation, athletic build, anything aspirational or promotional-looking
